text.title: Content Attribution Model Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every Content Attribution Model application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them. Plus a skip-methodology rule for decision-tree leaves that exit the methodology.

rule.statement: Apply to one content portfolio scope (blog OR podcast OR newsletter — not the mix in one record).
Every piece row carries publish_date (ISO), utm_source, conversions_first_touch (int), conversions_assisted (int), source path.
rule.rationale: Typed values make the rollup falsifiable.

rule.statement: Output names a single accountable owner. No 'content team' or 'we' as owner.
Semver + last_reviewed. Re-run when ≥10 new pieces published or attribution model changes.
Every ranking decision (top piece, deprecation candidate) carries a one-line rationale citing a specific number from the data.
rule.rationale: Rankings without numeric anchors are folklore.

rule.statement: Attribution window (e.g. 30-day post-touch, 90-day post-touch) declared up front and frozen for the report.
rule.rationale: Window adjustment after seeing data manufactures wins.

rule.statement: Skip this methodology when the decision tree resolves to a skip branch — preconditions are not met OR an adjacent methodology fits better.
rule.rationale: Skipping cleanly preserves the integrity of the rubric; misapplied methodologies produce false positives downstream.
